title: pyrola minor subsp. faurieana
intro: Pyrola minor subsp. faurieana, also known as wintergreen, is a subspecies of the Pyrola minor plant, a low-growing, evergreen perennial native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Pyrola minor subsp. faurieana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat skin conditions. Herbal preparations of wintergreen can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, although it is essential to use caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially in large quantities or for extended periods.
